bio: Mohammad Hanifnejad was an agricultural engineer and the principal co-founder of the [Mojahedin-e Khalq](/orgs/mojahedin-e-khalq) (MEK), established on 6 September 1965 alongside [Saeed Mohsen](/people/saeed-mohsen) and [Ali-Asghar Badizadegan](/people/ali-asghar-badizadegan). A former member of Mehdi Bazargan's Freedom Movement, he became convinced that a more radical approach was needed to challenge the Shah's regime. The three founders spent 1965–1969 recruiting cadres and developing the MEK's ideology, blending progressive Islam with anti-imperialist politics. He was arrested by SAVAK in 1971 and executed on 25 May 1972 along with his two co-founders.
editorial_notes: Executed by Shah's regime, not the Islamic Republic. Mentored Massoud Rajavi who joined MEK in 1967.
